reasoning:
The forecast evaluates whether CRL's closing price will be higher than its 2026-02-01 price (which effectively is the 2026-01-30 close of $210.48) on two upcoming dates: Feb 8 and March 3, 2026. 

1. Horizon 1 (2026-02-08): Resolving to the Feb 6 closing price, this represents a very short 5-day trading window. The stock has experienced strong downward momentum over the past week (dropping from $226.77 on Jan 21 down to $210.48 on Jan 30). In the short term, consecutive negative closes can have slight continuation, keeping the chance of immediately rebounding above this price just below 50%. The linear model's upward trend must be balanced against this sharp recent drop. (Forecast: 0.47)

2. Horizon 2 (2026-03-03): Resolving approximately a month out, this date falls after the upcoming Q4 earnings and FY2026 guidance call scheduled for February 18, 2026. A recent business update on January 12 noted the company expects "the top end of the guidance ranges for 2026 organic revenue growth will be at least flat," which is relatively weak but likely already priced into the January pullback. As the forecast horizon extends over an earnings event, individual stock predictability drops and reverts heavily to a coin toss, modulated slightly by the equity risk premium. Following the principles of forecasting, longer horizons face higher uncertainty and therefore the probability reverts toward the 0.50 baseline. (Forecast: 0.49)
